justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Chain timeout violation identified — output explicitly states 'worst-case OrderService latency (6s) also exceeds CartService's 5s timeout' in timeout_config and the retry math in the InventoryService interaction. (2) Missing circuit breaker on OrderService identified — called out in component_interactions and failure_modes. (3) PaymentService 8s > CartService 5s orphaned call risk identified — thoroughly covered in component_interactions, contract_compliance, and escalations. (4) Dual-write/partial completion risk identified — the OrderService succeeds but PaymentService fails scenario is covered in component_interactions (marked 'broken'), failure_modes, and escalated as a top-level integration risk. (5) Saga/compensation pattern recommended — explicitly recommended in failure_modes and escalations. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough chain analysis — did not assess services individually, analyzed the full synchronous call chain with specific timeout math
Identified additional risks beyond the rubric: double-reservation from InventoryService retry without idempotency, missing circuit breaker on PaymentService→gateway, cascading failure under load
Escalations are well-routed with clear business impact framing ('money-losing bug', 'cascading business failure')
Observability review is comprehensive — identified missing correlation IDs, distributed tracing, orphaned-order metrics, and circuit breaker state visibility
Operational readiness section includes practical remediation: data cleanup runbooks, reconciliation procedures, specific config changes needed
Confidence section is well-calibrated — explicitly states assumptions about idempotency keys and thread model rather than asserting unknowns as facts

trials.justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Chain timeout violation identified — OrderService retry window 6s > CartService 5s timeout explicitly called out. (2) Missing circuit breaker on OrderService identified — noted as unhandled failure mode with recommendation. (3) PaymentService 8s gateway timeout > CartService 5s timeout identified as ghost/orphaned payment risk. (4) Dual-write/partial completion risk identified — order created without payment captured, with no compensation transaction. (5) Saga/compensation pattern recommended as the fix. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional chain-level analysis — never falls into the trap of assessing services individually
Correctly identifies the timeout math as deterministic failure modes, not edge cases
Goes beyond rubric requirements: identifies idempotency gap for user retries, ghost payment scenario, and PaymentService missing its own circuit breaker on the gateway
Escalations are well-routed — timeout/saga to MJ (architect), observability to Shaq (implementer)
Provides concrete fix recommendations: timeout values (>=7s, >=9s), saga pattern, idempotency keys, reconciliation jobs
Observability review is thorough — correlation IDs, distributed tracing, structured logging for timeout events all flagged
Rollback plan identifies the non-obvious risk that rolling back individual services could worsen inconsistency

trials.justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Chain timeout violation identified — OrderService worst-case 6s exceeds CartService's 5s timeout, explicitly calculated as 3s+3s. (2) Missing circuit breaker on CartService→OrderService identified and flagged as asymmetry. (3) PaymentService 8s gateway timeout exceeding CartService 5s timeout identified as orphaned/ghost charge risk. (4) Dual-write/partial completion risk clearly identified — order created without payment, inventory reserved without fulfillment. (5) Saga/compensation pattern recommended explicitly. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough chain-level analysis — every interaction assessed as part of the full checkout flow, not in isolation
Ghost charge scenario articulated with precision: CartService times out, PaymentService continues, customer charged for 'failed' order
Concrete timeout remediation math provided (reduce InventoryService timeout to 2s for 4s total, or increase CartService timeout to 8s)
Identified secondary risks beyond the rubric: phantom inventory holds, missing correlation IDs, no rollback strategy, missing health check on CartService
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and business justification
Confidence section with explicit assumptions adds appropriate epistemic humility
